cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for export audit completeness on successful export
preconditions: Role-matrix fixture provides Lecturer, DepartmentAdmin, and AcademicAdmin users with known export scope.
Attendance data exists within each actor's authorized scope.
Audit log store is queryable via GET /v1/audit-logs for authorized roles.
steps: As Lecturer, complete POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and scoped filters; wait for job completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by actorUserId and export action type for the lecturer export.
Repeat export completion and audit query for DepartmentAdmin and AcademicAdmin within their scopes.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row (AC-17): every successful export across authorized roles produces exactly one audit log entry containing actor, timestamp, scope filter summary, and format csv (CMP-02, PRM-11).

cases.title: Export module persists immutable audit row on job completion at DB boundary
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance rows in a lecturer-assigned section.
Export service and audit repository use real DB transactions.
Export job processor can be driven to Completed state in test harness.
steps: Invoke export job creation and completion use-case for lecturer actor with filters { termId, classSectionId }.
Query AuditLog table directly for rows tied to the exportJobId or correlation ID.
Assert persisted fields: actorUserId, actor role, scope filter summary, format csv, and occurredAt timestamp.
cases.expected: Module integration commits an immutable AuditLog row when export completes; all minimum audit fields from permissions §7.1 (actor, role, scope filter, file format, timestamp) are present and queryable (FR-30, BR-22).

cases.title: HTTP contract for export completion audit via POST export and GET audit-logs
preconditions: Assigned lecturer is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Attendance data exists for lecturer-scoped classSectionId.
AcademicAdmin or SystemAuditor can query audit logs per role policy.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId, classSectionId } } and Idempotency-Key; wait until job status is Completed.
Send GET /v1/audit-logs?actorUserId={lecturerId}&actionType=Export&from={exportStart}&to={exportEnd}&page=1&pageSize=25.
Validate HTTP 200 audit response envelope and inspect the matching audit entry payload.
cases.expected: Export returns 202 with exportJobId; after completion, GET /v1/audit-logs returns 200 with standard envelope containing an entry whose fields include actorUserId, occurredAt timestamp, scope filter summary matching request filters, and format csv.

cases.title: Primary lecturer export workflow writes audit entry on completion (WF-07)
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to section SE101-01 with closed sessions containing attendance outcomes.
WF-07 reporting/export flow is exercisable end-to-end via API.
No prior export audit exists for the target filter set in the test window.
steps: Authenticate as lecturer assigned to SE101-01.
Request POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and filters covering the section.
Poll export job until Completed and download CSV artifact.
Query audit logs for export action by the lecturer within the request correlation window.
cases.expected: WF-07 steps G–H complete: export artifact is generated and AuditLog records actor, scope filter summary for SE101-01, format csv, and timestamp aligned with export completion (06-main-workflows §9.2–9.4).

cases.title: AcademicAdmin institution-scoped export audit records broad scope summary
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated with institution-wide export authority.
Attendance data spans multiple faculties and sections within institution scope.
Audit query access is available to AcademicAdmin.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and term-wide filters as AcademicAdmin.
Wait for export job completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for the AcademicAdmin export action.
Compare audit scope filter summary against request filters and role-resolved institution scope.
cases.expected: Successful institution-scoped export produces audit entry with AcademicAdmin as actor, format csv, timestamp, and scope summary reflecting institution-level filter context (not lecturer section-only scope).

cases.title: Denied export does not produce successful export audit entry
preconditions: Student user is authenticated; ExportJob.execute is denied per permissions matrix.
Attendance export data exists in the institution.
Audit log baseline count for export-completed actions is captured before test.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv filters as Student; confirm 403 denial.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs for export-completed actions tied to the Student actor and request correlation ID.
Verify no export job reached Completed state.
cases.expected: 403 denial returns with no exportJobId or completed job; no audit log entry exists with successful export semantics (actor Student, format csv, completed scope) for the denied request (AC-17 applies only to successful exports).

cases.title: Out-of-scope export rejection leaves no completed-export audit row
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to section A only is authenticated.
Section B attendance data exists outside lecturer scope.
Audit store is queryable before and after the denied request.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId } as lecturer.
Confirm 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden response with no exportJobId.
Query audit logs and export job persistence for any new completed-export audit tied to section B.
cases.expected: Denied out-of-scope export returns 403; no ExportCompleted audit row is written with section B scope or format csv for a job that never successfully completed.

cases.title: Idempotent export replay does not duplicate audit entries for one successful export
preconditions: Lecturer actor and scoped attendance data exist in integration DB.
POST /v1/exports/attendance idempotency returns existing exportJobId for duplicate Idempotency-Key per API §7.1.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with a fixed Idempotency-Key and wait for job completion.
Count AuditLog export rows for the exportJobId.
Replay the same POST with identical Idempotency-Key and body in parallel or sequence.
Re-count AuditLog export rows for the same exportJobId.
cases.expected: Exactly one audit log entry exists per successfully completed export job; idempotent replay returns the same exportJobId without creating duplicate completed-export audit rows.

cases.title: Lecturer browser export journey creates queryable audit trail after CSV download
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Reporting/export UI is available with scoped section filter and CSV export action.
AcademicAdmin or authorized auditor can access audit log UI or API.
steps: Navigate to attendance report or export page.
Select an assigned section, choose CSV format, and trigger export.
Confirm download or job-completed status in UI.
Open audit log view (or admin audit page) filtered to export actions and verify new entry for the lecturer with scope and format.
cases.expected: UI export completion is followed by a visible or queryable audit log entry showing actor, timestamp, scope filter summary, and csv format matching the export request (FLOW-12 step 4, AC-17).

cases.title: Failed export job processing does not write success audit before completion
preconditions: Integration environment can simulate export job failure after queueing (e.g. empty result set policy failure or processor error).
Lecturer-scoped export request is valid for authorization but fails during generation.
steps: Create export job via module use-case that transitions to Failed terminal state.
Query AuditLog for completed-export action type tied to the failed job ID.
Verify export job status remains Failed, not Completed.
cases.expected: No audit log entry with successful export semantics (completed scope, format csv) is persisted for a job that did not complete successfully; AC-17 requires audit only when export completes successfully.
